sanskrit:
यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति।
तदा गन्तासि निर्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च॥
transliteration:
yadā te moha-kalilaṃ buddhir vyatitariṣyati |
tadā gantāsi nirvedaṃ śrotavyasya śrutasya ca ||
literal_meaning: When your intelligence crosses the dense thicket of delusion, you will become indifferent to what has been heard and what is yet to be heard.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s cognitive fog (moha-kalila) is about to lift; once discriminative intellect (buddhi) pierces the tangle of conflicting scripts, the compulsive need to seek more information, approval, or authority collapses. A post-delusion quietude (nirveda) emerges—not apathy, but freedom from second-hand knowing.
psychological_state: Post-delusion disillusionment—intellectual emancipation from external validators and scriptural over-reliance; emergence of autonomous discernment.
trigger_scenario: A professional who has collected dozens of self-help books and online courses finally realizes that no new formula will dissolve their impostor syndrome; they stop consuming and quietly act.
anchor_text: When confusion clears, you stop chasing borrowed answers and rest in your own quiet knowing.
